2605.10876.title: AssayBench: An Assay-Level Virtual Cell Benchmark for LLMs and Agents
2605.10876.paper_summary: Recent advances in machine learning and large-scale biological data collections have revived the prospect of building a virtual cell, a computational model of cellular behavior that could accelerate biological discovery. One of the most compelling promises of this vision is the ability to perform in silico phenotypic screens, in which a model predicts the effects of cellular perturbations in unseen biological contexts. This task combines heterogeneous textual inputs with diverse phenotypic outputs, making it particularly well-suited to LLMs and agentic systems. Yet, no standard benchmark currently exists for this task, as existing efforts focus on narrower molecular readouts that are only indirectly aligned with the phenotypic endpoints driving many real-world drug discovery workflows. In this work, we present AssayBench, a benchmark for phenotypic screen prediction, built from 1,920 publicly available CRISPR screens spanning five broad classes of cellular phenotypes. We formulate the screen prediction task as a gene rank prediction for each screen and introduce the adjusted nDCG, a continuous metric for comparing performance across heterogeneous assays. Our extensive evaluation shows that existing methods remain far from empirically estimated performance ceilings and zero-shot generalist LLMs outperform biology-specific LLMs and trainable baselines. Optimization techniques such as fine-tuning, ensembling, and prompt optimization can further improve LLM performance on this task. Overall, AssayBench offers a practical testbed for measuring progress toward in silico phenotypic screening and, more broadly, virtual cell models.
2605.10876.paper_summary_zh: 最近在機器學習和大規模生物數據收集方面的進展，重新點燃了構建虛擬細胞的前景，這是一種細胞行為的計算模型，能加速生物學發現。<lb/>這一願景最引人注目的承諾之一是能夠進行計算機模擬的表型篩選，其中模型預測在未見的生物背景中細胞擾動的影響。<lb/>這項任務結合了異質的文本輸入和多樣的表型輸出，使其特別適合於LLMs和代理系統。<lb/>然而，目前尚不存在針對這項任務的標準基準，因為現有的努力集中於較狹窄的分子讀數，這些讀數僅與驅動許多現實世界藥物發現工作流程的表型終點間接對齊。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們提出了AssayBench，一個用於表型篩選預測的基準，該基準由1,920個公開可用的CRISPR篩選組成，涵蓋五大類細胞表型。<lb/>我們將篩選預測任務表述為每個篩選的基因排名預測，並引入了調整後的nDCG，這是一種用於比較異質檢測性能的連續度量。<lb/>我們的廣泛評估顯示，現有方法仍然遠未達到經驗估計的性能上限，而零樣本通用LLMs的表現超過了生物學特定的LLMs和可訓練的基準。<lb/>優化技術如微調、集成和提示優化可以進一步提高LLM在這項任務上的性能。<lb/>總的來說，AssayBench提供了一個實用的測試平台，用於衡量向計算機模擬表型篩選的進展，以及更廣泛的虛擬細胞模型。
2605.10876.author: Edward De Brouwer et.al.
2605.10876.authors: Edward De Brouwer, Carl Edwards, Alexander Wu, Jenna Collier, Graham Heimberg, Xiner Li, Meena Subramaniam, Ehsan Hajiramezanali, David Richmond, Jan-Christian Hütter, Sara Mostafavi, Gabriele Scalia
